title: Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao

seo.title: How to Make Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao — Easy Chinese Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao. This easy Chinese recipe includes step-by-step instructions, tips, and serving ideas.
longTailKeywords: how to make Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao
easy Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao recipe
authentic Chinese Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao

Chinese Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao recipe
seo.h1: Authentic Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao Recipe
h2Headings: What is Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao?

seo.imageAlt: Authentic Chinese Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao recipe
seo.imageCaption: Delicious homemade Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao — a beloved Chinese classic

description: Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao is a beloved Hong Kong classic, famous for its soft, fluffy bread base and a unique sweet, crumbly topping that resembles a pineapple's texture. Despite its name, this iconic pastry traditionally contains no pineapple, offering a delightful contrast of textures and flavors. Enjoy this comforting and slightly sweet bun as a perfect snack or breakfast.

ingredients.notes: cold, cut into cubes, for the topping

instructions.title: Prepare the Dough
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine flour, sugar, yeast, and salt. Add warm milk and beaten egg, mixing until a shaggy dough forms. Knead on a lightly floured surface for 8-10 minutes until smooth and elastic. Incorporate softened butter and continue kneading until butter is fully absorbed and the dough is very smooth. Place in a greased bowl, cover, and let rise in a warm place for 1-1.5 hours, or until doubled in size.

instructions.title: Make the Pineapple Topping
instructions.text: While the dough is rising, prepare the topping. In a separate bowl, whisk together flour, powdered sugar, and baking powder. Cut in the cold butter using your fingertips or a pastry blender until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs. In a small bowl, combine egg yolk and milk, then add to the flour mixture. Mix until just combined to form a soft dough. Do not overmix. Wrap the topping dough in plastic wrap and chill in the refrigerator for at least 30 minutes.

instructions.title: Shape the Buns
instructions.text: Once the dough has doubled, gently punch it down and divide into 8 equal portions. Roll each portion into a smooth ball. Place the dough balls on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper, leaving space between them. For the topping, divide the chilled topping dough into 8 equal portions. Roll each portion into a thin disc, about 4-5 inches in diameter.

instructions.title: Apply the Topping and Second Proof
instructions.text: Carefully place one topping disc over each dough ball, gently pressing down to adhere it. The topping should cover about two-thirds of the bun, leaving some dough exposed at the bottom. Use a knife or dough scraper to lightly score a crisscross pattern on the topping surface. Cover the buns loosely with plastic wrap and let them rise in a warm place for another 30-45 minutes, or until noticeably puffy.

instructions.title: Bake the Pineapple Buns
instructions.text: Preheat your oven to 180°C (350°F). Just before baking, brush the scored topping surface with the beaten egg wash. This will give the buns a golden, shiny finish. Bake for 15-20 minutes, or until the topping is golden brown and the buns are cooked through. Keep an eye on them to prevent over-browning.

instructions.title: Cool and Serve
instructions.text: Once baked, remove the Pineapple Buns from the oven and transfer them to a wire rack to cool slightly. These buns are best enjoyed warm, ideally with a slice of cold butter inserted into the middle for an extra decadent treat, a popular way to serve them in Hong Kong.

tips: Do not overmix the topping dough; a crumbly texture is desired for the iconic pineapple-like crust. Overmixing can make it tough.
For an extra crispy topping, ensure your butter for the topping is very cold when you cut it into the flour mixture.

Pineapple buns are best enjoyed fresh and warm. If storing, keep them at room temperature in an airtight container for up to 2 days, or freeze for longer storage. Reheat gently in an oven or microwave.
article: Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao is a beloved Chinese Baking dish enjoyed across Chinese. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
